name: Brown-Sugar Caramel
description: Lean brown-sugar caramel - brown sugar melted with skim milk and a pinch of salt, NO butter (William's tested version: tastes great and drops the fat). Genuine caramelized brown-sugar flavor (you cannot caramelize Splenda), kept lean. Reusable across anything that needs caramel - cook it to the texture you want: saucy, thick, or caramel-apple-thick (see the finishing step). Sub-recipe, 1 batch, ~81 cal.

steps.instructionText: Combine the brown sugar, skim milk, and salt in a small saucepan and cook over medium, stirring, until the sugar fully dissolves and the syrup bubbles and thickens slightly (~2-3 min). No butter - the skim milk alone keeps it lean. Cool slightly; it thickens as it sits, so rewarm to drizzle.

FINISH TO YOUR TEXTURE (reusable - take it only as far as you need): SAUCY/pourable - stop as soon as the sugar is fully dissolved and smooth; it coats a spoon thinly and pours in a thread, for drizzle (~225F). THICK/spoonable - keep on low and reduce 1-2 min more until it mounds and falls in a slow ribbon; sets like dulce de leche and holds a swirl (~235-240F, soft-ball). CARAMEL-APPLE THICK/chewy - reduce further until a drop in cold water forms a soft pliable ball (~245-250F, firm-ball); off heat it clings and sets tacky-chewy. No thermometer? Cold-water drop test - thin thread = saucy, soft ball = spoonable, firmer ball = chewy.
